name: PC Automation
description: Full PC control: mouse, keyboard, and window management. Use with WEBCAM.desktop_screenshot for a see-and-act loop.
routing_instructions: VISION/AUTOMATION SYNERGY: You DO have 'eyes'! You can interact with the user's screen. When asked to click on something, FIRST call WEBCAM__desktop_screenshot to perceive the screen. THEN, instead of guessing (x, y) coordinates, use AUTOMATION__click_text to deterministically find and click labeled buttons, tabs, or menus. Only use AUTOMATION__click with coordinates for targets without text.

commands.get_screen_size: Returns the screen resolution (width x height).
commands.click_text: Find text on screen using OCR and click it.
commands.scroll: Scroll wheel at (x, y) — positive=up, negative=down.
commands.drag: Click-and-drag from one position to another.
commands.type_text: Simulate keyboard typing of a text string.
commands.press_key: Press and release a single key (enter, esc, f5...).
commands.focus_window: Bring a window to foreground by title.
commands.get_browser_tabs: List all open browser tabs via Windows Accessibility API. Use before focus_browser_tab.
commands.focus_browser_tab: Click a browser tab by title fragment (no coordinates needed).
commands.read_window_controls: Read the UI control tree of any application window.

function.name: AUTOMATION__get_screen_size
function.description: Returns the current screen resolution in pixels (width x height). Use to determine valid coordinate boundaries before clicking.

function.name: AUTOMATION__get_mouse_position
function.description: Returns the current mouse cursor position as (x, y) pixel coordinates.

function.name: AUTOMATION__move_to
function.description: Smoothly moves the mouse cursor to the specified absolute screen coordinates.

duration.description: Movement duration in seconds (0 = instant). Default: 0.15.

function.name: AUTOMATION__click_text
function.description: Scans the screen using local OCR to find a specific text string and clicks on its exact bounding box center. Prefer this over guessing x, y coordinates when trying to click labeled buttons, tabs, or menus.

text.description: The exact word or phrase to find on the screen, e.g. 'YouTube' or 'Send'.

instance.description: If multiple matches exist, which one to click (0 = first match). Default: 0.

button.description: Which mouse button to click. Default: 'left'.

function.name: AUTOMATION__click
function.description: Clicks the mouse at the specified screen coordinates. Use desktop_screenshot first to identify the target position.

button.description: Which mouse button to click. Default: 'left'.

function.name: AUTOMATION__scroll
function.description: Scrolls the mouse wheel at the specified position. Positive clicks scroll up, negative scroll down.

x.description: Horizontal pixel coordinate of scroll position.

y.description: Vertical pixel coordinate of scroll position.

clicks.description: Number of scroll steps. Positive = up, negative = down.

function.name: AUTOMATION__drag
function.description: Performs a click-and-drag from one screen position to another. Useful for moving files, selecting text, or adjusting sliders.

function.name: AUTOMATION__type_text
function.description: Simulates keyboard typing of the given text, character by character. Does NOT press Enter — use type_and_enter for that.

interval.description: Delay in seconds between each keystroke. Default: 0.02.

function.name: AUTOMATION__press_key
function.description: Presses and releases a single keyboard key. Valid keys include: enter, esc, tab, space, backspace, delete, up, down, left, right, home, end, f1-f12, ctrl, alt, shift, win.

function.name: AUTOMATION__hotkey
function.description: Executes a keyboard shortcut by pressing multiple keys simultaneously. Common examples: ['ctrl','c'] (copy), ['ctrl','v'] (paste), ['ctrl','z'] (undo), ['alt','f4'] (close window), ['ctrl','shift','esc'] (task manager).

keys.description: Ordered list of keys to hold together (e.g. ['ctrl', 'c']).

function.name: AUTOMATION__type_and_enter
function.description: Types the given text and immediately presses Enter. Use for submitting forms, executing terminal commands, or confirming dialogs.

text.description: The text to type before pressing Enter.

function.name: AUTOMATION__list_windows
function.description: Returns a list of all currently visible window titles. Always call this before focus_window to find the exact window title.

function.name: AUTOMATION__focus_window
function.description: Brings a window to the foreground using a partial, case-insensitive title match. Use list_windows first to identify the correct title.

function.name: AUTOMATION__minimize_window
function.description: Minimizes a window to the taskbar using a partial title match.

function.name: AUTOMATION__maximize_window
function.description: Maximizes a window to fill the screen using a partial title match.

function.name: AUTOMATION__get_browser_tabs
function.description: Returns titles of all currently open browser tabs (Chrome, Edge, Opera, Brave) using the Windows Accessibility API. No screenshot needed. Call this FIRST when the user asks to switch to a browser tab.

function.name: AUTOMATION__focus_browser_tab
function.description: Clicks on a browser tab whose title contains the given fragment. Uses Windows Accessibility API — no coordinate guessing. Call AUTOMATION__get_browser_tabs first to verify the correct tab title.

title_fragment.description: Part of the tab title to match (case-insensitive), e.g. 'YouTube' or 'Wikipedia'.

function.name: AUTOMATION__read_window_controls
function.description: Reads all interactive controls (buttons, fields, links) from an application window's accessibility tree. Use to understand a GUI's structure before clicking, no screenshot needed.

title_fragment.description: Part of the window title to target, e.g. 'Notepad' or 'Chrome'.
